metadata.title:
TO PLACE TELEPHONES


UPON The UNFAIR LIST

pages.raw_text:
Unless the Pacific States Telephone
Telegraph company agrees to
peace conference with the striking
operators by Monday noon, 1t will be
placed on the unfair" list and gen.
eral boycott established against the
company by union labor on account
Of It's attitude towards Its former
employees.


The young ladies will then busy
themselves securing signatures OF
telephone subscribers to orders re-
questing the removal Of telephones
from residences and business houses
1t Is expected that enough Of these
removal orders will be secured to
compel the company to come to terms.
as has been done In other cities.


When the company has the option
OF removing thousand or SO phones
OF comforting with its operators, the
strikers confidently expect that II will
prefer to keep'its phones and grant
some Of the girls demands.


In ~ Of the latter. the removal
orders WI be returned to the signers


No desertions are reported from the
ranks Of thu strikers and never was
there e. IA Of strikers who have
carried On a moro persistent or faith
full pjeket duty than the telephone
girls Now on strike. They stay with
their posts like veterans and are alert
every minute OF the time to intercept
and persuade the girls now in the
employ Of the company to Join them.
